title: Silo, pool, and bridge isolation trade-offs
description: Silo gives each tenant dedicated resources for strong isolation at higher cost, pool shares them for density, and bridge mixes the two.
body: Use when reasoning about which isolation model fits a tenant. Do not treat the choice as purely a cost decision. Silo isolates a tenant in its own resources, maximizing separation and blast-radius containment but costing more per tenant; pool shares resources with logical separation for density but concentrates risk; bridge assigns sensitive tenants silos and others pools. Inspect each tenant's sensitivity and scale. Validation signals include the model matching each tenant's isolation requirement. Risk boundary: pooling a highly sensitive tenant trades isolation for cost in a way that is hard to undo, so map sensitivity to model deliberately.
